source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The simplest model for low-lying states of nuclei with $N$ and $Z$ between 20 and 28 involves only $f_{7/2}$ nucleons. The first excited state in ${ }_{21}^{43}\text{Ca}_{23}$ is shown below in Fig. 2.14 with a half-life of 34 picoseconds for decay to the ground state. Estimate the lifetime expected for this state on the basis of a single-particle model. The experimental values are \( \begin{array}{ll} \mu_{n}=-1.91 \mu_{N}, & \mu\left({ }^{41} Ca\right)=-1.59 \mu_{N} \ \mu_{p}=2.79 \mu_{N}, & \mu\left({ }^{41} Sc\right)=5.43 \mu_{N} \end{array} \)

decision_reasons: question, answer, and required image are all present with no registry issues
high image dependency is supported by an existing image asset, so core semantics appear recoverable
verifiability is strong because the sample has a short-text answer and passed asset registry checks
